content.problemStatement:
Competitive Programmers love concise statements. Today, we have one such problem for you!

You're given an array AAA with NNN integers, A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​. You have to partition the elements of array AAA into exactly KKK disjoint subsets such that the following conditions are satisfied:


Size of each subset must be at least 222.
Let the KKK subsets be S1,S2,…,SKS_1, S_2, \dots, S_KS1​,S2​,…,SK​. Let Di=max⁡(Si)−min⁡(Si)D_i = \max(S_i) - \min(S_i)Di​=max(Si​)−min(Si​) where max⁡(Si)\max(S_i)max(Si​) is the maximum element in SiS_iSi​ and min⁡(Si)\min(S_i)min(Si​) is the minimum element in SiS_iSi​. We want to minimize the value of D1+D2+D3+⋯+DKD_1 + D_2 + D_3 + \dots + D_KD1​+D2​+D3​+⋯+DK​.
Find the minimum possible sum of D1+D2+D3+⋯+DKD_1 + D_2 + D_3 + \dots + D_KD1​+D2​+D3​+⋯+DK​ that you can obtain.


Input Format
The first line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of the test cases.


The first line of each test case will contain two space-separated integers N,KN, KN,K.


The next line will contain NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​.



Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer denoting the minimum sum.
constraints: 2 ≤ Sum of N over all test cases in a single file ≤ 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of the test cases. The first line of each test case will contain two space-separated integers N, K. The next line will contain N space-separated integers A1, A2, A3, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer denoting the minimum sum of D1 + D2 + D3 + ⋯ + DK that you can obtain.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The best partition that we can obtain involves partitioning A into {16, 5, 17} and {19, 23}, incurring a cost of (17 - 5) + (23 - 19) = 16.
The best partition that we can obtain involves partitioning A into {1, 2} and {3, 4}, incurring a cost of (2 - 1) + (4 - 3) = 2.
The best partition that we can obtain involves partitioning A into {1, 2}, {3, 4}, and {5, 6}, incurring a cost of (2 - 1) + (4 - 3) + (6 - 5) = 3.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first sort the array A in ascending order. Then, we initialize two pointers, one at the beginning of the array and one at the end of the array. We calculate the difference between the maximum and minimum elements in each subset and add it to the total sum. We use a greedy approach to minimize the sum by always choosing the smallest possible difference.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) — due to sorting the array for each test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — for storing the sorted array

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, K = map(int, input().split())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    A.sort()
    sum = 0
    for i in range(K):
        sum += A[N - i - 1] - A[2 * i]
    print(sum)
